channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: House Oversight Chair James Comer Dismisses Trump's Reflecting Pool Vandalism Claims as Low Priority
item.description: House Oversight Committee Chairman James Comer (R-KY) stated that his panel has no plans to investigate the alleged vandalism of the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool, a topic President Donald Trump ha...

item.title: Poll Finds Many Americans Doubt White House Transparency About President Trump's Health
item.description: A recent Quinnipiac University poll suggests that a significant portion of the American public questions the transparency of the Trump administration regarding President Donald Trump's health. Accordi...

item.title: Republican Leaders Seek Resolution After Trump Delays Bipartisan Housing Bill Over Election Legislation Demands
item.description: House Speaker Mike Johnson and other Republican leaders were working to resolve internal tensions after President Donald Trump unexpectedly halted plans to sign a bipartisan housing bill. According to...

item.title: George Michael’s Artistic Reinvention During the Listen Without Prejudice Vol. 1 Era
item.description: The article revisits a series of promotional photographs taken of George Michael in 1990 during the release of his album “Listen Without Prejudice Vol. 1.” Following the enormous commercial success of...

item.title: Opinion piece examines patriotic music, Trump rallies, and Lee Greenwood’s “God Bless the USA”
item.description: This opinion article analyzes the political and cultural meaning of the patriotic song “God Bless the USA,” performed by Lee Greenwood, in the context of Donald Trump’s public events and political mes...

item.title: Kash Patel’s partner performs national anthem at Trump 250th anniversary rally, prompting online debate
item.description: Alexis Wilkins, a 27-year-old country music artist and the girlfriend of FBI Director Kash Patel, performed the U.S. national anthem at President Donald Trump’s “Great American State Fair,” an event t...

item.title: House GOP cancels scheduled votes amid internal party dispute over stalled legislation
item.description: House Republican leaders were forced to cancel a planned Friday voting session after an internal standoff within the party threatened to disrupt the chamber’s legislative schedule. According to a noti...

item.title: Disney to Pay $50 Million Settlement Over Alleged Price Inflation Impacting YouTube TV and DIRECTV Stream Users
item.description: Disney has agreed to a $50 million settlement following a class action lawsuit involving subscribers of YouTube TV and DIRECTV Stream. The lawsuit alleges that Disney used its market power to force st...

item.title: ICE Agents Confront Syracuse Poll Worker Over Instagram Post at Polling Location
item.description: Two U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) agents entered a polling site in Syracuse, New York, during the June 2026 primary election to confront poll worker Paigelynne Gonyea about a social m...

item.title: Internal Republican divisions resurface over same-sex marriage and LGBTQ issues
item.description: A new political dispute within the Republican Party has emerged over same-sex marriage and LGBTQ-related issues, highlighting ongoing ideological divisions a decade after the Supreme Court’s Obergefel...

item.title: Cotswolds property market cools as house prices decline from post-pandemic highs
item.description: The housing market in England’s Cotswolds region, long considered one of the country’s most desirable rural areas, is showing clear signs of cooling after years of rapid growth. According to recent an...

item.title: A Look at Google’s Former Interview Brain Teasers and Why They Were Discontinued
item.description: This article revisits a well-known phase in Google’s hiring history when the company famously used unconventional “brainteaser” questions during job interviews. In the 2000s and early 2010s, candidate...

item.title: Chronopharmacology and the Importance of Medication Timing in Treatment Effectiveness
item.description: This article explains the principles of chronopharmacology, the branch of pharmacology that studies how the effectiveness and safety of medications can vary depending on the time of day they are admin...

item.title: Gianna Maria Canale and Her Legacy in 1950s Italian Cinema
item.description: Gianna Maria Canale (1927–2009) was a prominent Italian actress who became one of the most recognizable screen figures of the 1950s, known for her commanding presence and striking physical appearance....

item.title: Opinion: Mitch McConnell's Long-Term Political Influence Helped Shape the Conditions That Enabled Trump's Rise
item.description: In this opinion article, columnist John Casey argues that former Senate Republican leader Mitch McConnell bears significant responsibility for many of the political and institutional developments that...

item.title: Joe Manganiello Details Seven-Year Autoimmune Health Struggle Ahead of Memoir Release
item.description: Actor Joe Manganiello has revealed that he spent the last seven years battling a series of severe autoimmune-related illnesses, a struggle he discusses in his upcoming memoir, Bloodlines. According to...

item.title: Sydney Gallego Rebuts Katie Miller's Claims About Her Family and Corrects Timeline of Relationship With Sen. Ruben Gallego
item.description: A public dispute unfolded on social media after Katie Miller, wife of White House Deputy Chief of Staff Stephen Miller and a conservative podcast host, criticized Senator Ruben Gallego of Arizona over...

item.title: How Pop-Culture Lunchboxes Became Schoolyard Status Symbols in the 1970s
item.description: The article explores the cultural significance of metal lunchboxes in American schools during the 1970s. More than simple containers for carrying meals, lunchboxes became highly visible status symbols...

item.title: Appeals Court Reconsiders Contempt Inquiry Into Trump Administration's Handling of Deportation Flights
item.description: A legal dispute involving the Trump administration and the federal judiciary is gaining renewed attention after a federal appeals court agreed to reconsider whether a contempt investigation should pro...

item.title: Leonard Cohen Estate Blocks Use of 'Hallelujah' at Trump's Freedom 250 Rally
item.description: The estate of late Canadian singer-songwriter Leonard Cohen publicly opposed and prevented the use of his famous song 'Hallelujah' at President Donald Trump's Freedom 250 rally held on June 24, 2026, ...
